strategy_description: Frankfurt-style IV scalping on all ten VEVs with BS vega at fitted smile IV; per-strike clip capped by vega rail (700) minus |pos|; partial extract delta hedge and two-sided extract + hydrogel MM. TTE: csv day 0/1/2 -> DTE 8/7/6 at open with intraday wind per plot_iv_smile_round3.
Parameter sweep: IV_SCALPING_THR 0.35, VEGA_RAIL 900, weaker hedge, no hydrogel. Intended to unlock more VEV flow; deep wings traded and total PnL collapsed.
Sweep: restore IV gate 0.7, skip trading when quiet (no flatten branch), ATM band 0.98<=K/S<=1.02 only, VEGA_RAIL 800, no hydrogel. Few VEV fills; extract MM still negative.

Parent v0 with merge_orders on extract/hydrogel to respect 200 limit when hedge and MM stack; VEGA_RAIL grid point 750. Best aggregate in this batch; day 2 still dominated by hydrogel + extract losses.

Parent v3 with MM drag removed (EXTRACT_MM_Q=0, HYDRO_MM_Q=0), lower hedge frac (0.12), higher VEGA_RAIL=850 to test whether pure vega-rail scalp can stand alone. Trades almost only in day 2; near-flat total.
Parent v3 without extract/hydro MM (pure vega-rail focus), VEGA_RAIL=650 and THR_OPEN=0.35 sweep. Small positive result from sparse ATM voucher fills on day 2; best in-branch so far.
Parent v5: ATM-only vouchers 0.97<=K/S<=1.03, VEGA_RAIL=700; no extract/hydro MM. Slightly above flat (+2) vs v5; still only day 2 activity.
Parent v5: IV_SCALPING_THR=0.55, THR_OPEN=0.30 sweep to engage main branch more. Small losses on v5500 + extract hedge; total -22. Worse than v5.
